TranslationContext: This file contains strings from Unit Deeds and acheivements.

Translations.Comment: Refers to unwavering resolve in battle; maintains the heroic tone of the achievement.
Refers to morale/break tests in wargaming. 'Prueba de Resistencia' is the standard terminology.
Classic fantasy achievement; maintains epic fantasy tone.
Direct and clear description of the deed achieved.
Conveys combat prowess and danger; serious wargame tone.
Clear description; 'mató' used for direct combat kills.
Emphasizes skill with ranged weapons; maintains martial tone.

Translations.Translation: Mató 1 enemigo con armas a distancia.
Translations.Comment: Distinguishes ranged combat from melee; appropriate terminology for wargaming.
Simple and direct; relates to dice roll fortune in wargaming.

Translations.Key: Rolled 1 natural six (33 % of all rolls).
Translations.Translation: Rodó 1 seis natural (33 % de todos los dados).
Translations.Comment: Preserves wargaming dice terminology; 'natural' indicates unmodified roll.
'Conquistador' conveys capturing objectives in battle; more evocative than direct 'anotador'.
Standard wargaming terminology; clear and direct.
Epic fantasy terminology; maintains consistency with other 'Slayer' titles.
Direct deed description.
Refers to wood elf or tree-like creatures; maintains fantasy tone with compound form.
'Derribó' is the appropriate verb for felling tree creatures; 'arbóreo' maintains fantasy tone.
Maintains the fantasy-meets-prehistoric tone of the original English.
Direct and clear; 'dinosaurio' is standard Spanish term.
Maintains the original's blend of fantasy and mechanical elements.
'Destruyó' is more appropriate than 'mató' for machines; wargaming terminology.
Classic fantasy hunter title; maintains epic tone.
'Mató' or 'Derrotó' both work; 'demonio mayor' emphasizes the creature's power.
Direct translation; maintains the martial prestige of this achievement.
Preserves capitalization for military rank; indicates high-value target defeated.
Idiomatic translation; 'lanzador' conveys the action of casting spells in combat.
Standard fantasy terminology; 'lanzó' is the perfect past for casting spells.
Direct translation; reflects the shameful nature of this 'achievement'.

Translations.Key: Fled off the battlefield with zero casualties.
Translations.Translation: Huyó del campo de batalla sin pérdidas.
Translations.Comment: 'Huyó' emphasizes shameful retreat; 'sin pérdidas' means no losses sustained.
Counterpart to 'Afortunado'; relates to poor dice rolls in wargaming.

Translations.Translation: Rodó 1 uno (33 % de todos los dados).
Translations.Comment: Mirrors the 'Lucky' achievement structure; 'uno' refers to lowest die result.
Heroic achievement; 'en pie' emphasizes last survivor's defiant stance.
'Miniatura' or 'modelo' both used in wargaming context; preserves game terminology.
Darkly humorous achievement; straightforward translation.
Clear wargaming reference; 'turno' is standard Spanish term for game turn.
Ironic title for useless unit; maintains humorous tone of the original.

Translations.Key: Killed no enemies, lost no models, took no objectives.
Translations.Translation: No mató enemigos, no perdió miniaturas, no capturó objetivos.
Translations.Comment: Parallel structure emphasizes complete uselessness; wargaming terminology preserved.
Tactical military term; demonstrates strategic acumen in combat.
Preserves wargaming terminology; 'carga' (charge) is a key tactical action.
Plural form; refers to the unit or group that achieved dragon kills.
Plural form emphasizing the skilled ranged unit.
Plural form; maintains consistency with singular version.
Plural form; maintains the compound structure.
Plural form maintaining the fantasy-prehistoric theme.
Plural form; preserves the military-mechanical theme.
Plural form; epic fantasy terminology.
Plural form; straightforward translation maintaining prestige.
Plural form; maintains the magical warrior aesthetic.
Plural form; maintains the shameful tone.
Escalated version; maintains terminology consistency.
Escalated achievement; demonstrates exceptional prowess.
Escalated combat achievement; mirrors the singular form.

Translations.Translation: Mató 3 enemigos con armas a distancia.
Translations.Comment: Escalated ranged achievement; demonstrates superior marksmanship.
Exclamatory title showing divine favor; contrasts with 'Cursed!' achievement.

Translations.Key: Rolled 3 natural sixes (33 % of all rolls).
Translations.Translation: Rodó 3 seises naturales (33 % de todos los dados).
Translations.Comment: Escalated lucky rolls; 'seises' is plural of the number six in wargaming context.
Escalated tactical achievement; demonstrates strategic dominance.
Escalated achievement; extraordinary feat of strength.
Escalated version; demonstrates dominance over nature-based creatures.
Escalated prehistoric achievement; shows mastery over ancient beasts.
Escalated mechanical destruction; demonstrates anti-armor effectiveness.
Escalated supernatural achievement; demonstrates divine favor or heroic power.
Escalated military achievement; slaying enemy officers shows exceptional valor.
Escalated magical achievement; demonstrates sorcerous prowess.
Exclamatory title showing ill fortune; direct opposite of 'Blessed!'.

Translations.Translation: Rodó 3 unos (33 % de todos los dados).
Translations.Comment: Escalated unlucky rolls; mirror of the blessed dice achievement.
Escalated tactical achievement; shows sustained strategic brilliance.

Translations.Key: Rolled 3 natural sixes (100 % of all rolls).
Translations.Translation: Rodó 3 seises naturales (100 % de todos los dados).
Translations.Comment: Perfect luck achievement; every roll was maximum—impossible without divine intervention or perfect fortune.

Translations.Translation: Rodó 3 unos (100 % de todos los dados).
Translations.Comment: Catastrophic curse; every roll was minimum—represents complete failure and misfortune.

Translations.Translation: Mató 13 enemigos con armas a distancia.
Translations.Comment: Ultimate ranged achievement; 13 is a lucky/unlucky number that emphasizes the extraordinary feat.
